headlines.title: Married at First Sight UK rape allegations serious, says government
headlines.description: A BBC Panorama investigation revealed allegations that two women had been raped during filming.

headlines.title: Watch: Channel 4 boss asked if she will apologise to MAFS UK women
headlines.description: The broadcaster's chief executive Priya Dogra walked away from an interview when given the opportunity to say sorry.

headlines.title: New High Street crime unit to target gangs fronting shops after BBC investigation
headlines.description: It follows BBC News exposing drug gangs, money laundering, immigration crime, and ghost directors linked to shop fronts.

headlines.title: Wrongly jailed Malkinson says demand for legal fees is 'penny-pinching'
headlines.description: Andrew Malkinson, who was wrongly jailed for rape, says being asked to repay legal fees is “penny-pinching”.

headlines.title: Ebola may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
headlines.description: Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

headlines.title: Man City set to replace Guardiola with Maresca
headlines.description: Enzo Maresca is set to replace Pep Guardiola as Manchester City manager before next season.

headlines.title: Jackson Pollock painting sells for record $181m at auction
headlines.description: The Abstract Expressionist's Number 7A, 1948 painting is now his most expensive artwork ever sold at auction.

headlines.title: UK unemployment rate unexpectedly rises
headlines.description: The number of UK job vacancies also fell to its lowest level in five years as the initial impact of the Iran war on businesses starts to be seen.

headlines.title: Selling children to survive: Afghan fathers forced to make impossible choices
headlines.description: In Afghanistan today, a staggering three in four people cannot meet their basic needs.

headlines.title: Swinney defends food prices policy ahead of first minister vote
headlines.description: The SNP leader says the proposed price cap on basic foodstuffs is not intended to force a fight with the UK government.

headlines.title: Junior school pupil treated for meningitis in fourth Reading case
headlines.description: The UKHSA says the fourth case has links with the same wider social network as the others.

headlines.title: 'Untouchable' teen motorbike thieves mocking victims on TikTok
headlines.description: BBC investigation finds hundreds of videos showing self-confessed thieves posing with stolen bikes.

headlines.title: Is a bank holiday heatwave on the way?
headlines.description: The warmest weather of the year is forecast for later this week after early rain.

headlines.title: Widows left in pension limbo after husbands' deaths
headlines.description: Pension payments have been delayed after Capita took over administration of the civil servants' scheme.

headlines.title: What really holds China and Russia together
headlines.description: The relationship endures despite a power imbalance, with both sides recognising that it is too important to fail.

headlines.title: Guardiola's legacy will shape the future of football in England for years to come
headlines.description: As Manchester City prepare for Pep Guardiola's exit, Phil McNulty reflects on how he has changed football in England.

headlines.title: Why people are so excited about Swatch's Royal Pop watch
headlines.description: A watch range by Swatch and luxury brand Audemars Piguet is causing a frenzy as people queue for days to get their hands on one.

headlines.title: The Republican who stood up to Trump is hoping for a big win against president
headlines.description: Kentucky Congressman Thomas Massie, who has crossed Trump on federal spending and the Epstein files, faces Republican voters.

headlines.title: What is Ebola and why is stopping this outbreak so difficult?
headlines.description: An outbreak of Ebola in DR Congo involves a rare strain and is in an area affected by conflict.

headlines.title: Teen suspects fatally shoot three in suspected hate crime at San Diego mosque
headlines.description: The alleged attackers were aged 17 and 18 and one left a note containing "generalised hate rhetoric", investigators say.

headlines.title: MP failed by NHS appointed first maternity adviser
headlines.description: Michelle Welsh says she will work to push for "better, safer care for mothers, babies and families".

headlines.title: British ex-soldier imprisoned by Russia says he feels abandoned in occupied Ukraine
headlines.description: In letters sent to the BBC from prison, Hayden Davies says he has had no contact with British authorities.

headlines.title: Two bodies of missing Italians recovered from inside Maldives cave
headlines.description: The two were among four divers located in the furthest chamber in the cave and their bodies were recovered by specialist divers.

headlines.title: Two dead in Spain shooting, with babies reportedly among injured
headlines.description: Police say a 25-year-old man is suspected of killing his parents and injuring four others in southern Spain.

headlines.title: Starbucks Korea sacks CEO over controversial 'Tank Day' promotion
headlines.description: Starbucks withdrew the campaign for its drink tumblers after many said it referenced a bloody crackdown.

headlines.title: 'Digi-hogs' tracked in bid to slow decline in hedgehog numbers
headlines.description: Researchers hope the project will also help to identify barriers preventing hedgehogs from finding food and mates in the wild.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Does Labour’s Leadership Race Mean A Battle Over Brexit?
headlines.description: Wes Streeting and Andy Burnham have said they want the UK to rejoin the EU.

headlines.title: Americanswers... on 5 Live! Has Zohran Mamdani achieved a miracle in New York?
headlines.description: The new mayor says he’s rescued NYC from its budget crisis

headlines.title: Man City set to replace Guardiola with Maresca
headlines.description: Enzo Maresca is set to replace Pep Guardiola as Manchester City manager before next season.

headlines.title: Stewart & Curtis in Scotland World Cup squad
headlines.description: Ross Stewart and Findlay Curtis are named in Scotland's World Cup squad but there is no place for Lennon Miller.

headlines.title: Could history be rewritten? Guardiola, Man City and the 115 charges
headlines.description: With Pep Guardiola expected to leave Manchester City at the end of this season, how do the club's 115 Premier League charges for alleged financial rule breaches potentially impact his legacy at the Etihad Stadium?

headlines.title: 'I broke the curse' - the fall and rise of a teenage Grand Slam champion
headlines.description: When 19-year-old Bianca Andreescu stunned Serena Williams to win the US Open. It was unthinkable it would be more than 2,300 days before her next triumph.

headlines.title: D-Day for Spygate: What's at stake and what could happen?
headlines.description: Southampton's place in the play-off final is at stake as they face a disciplinary hearing as they stand charged of watching a Middlesbrough training session. The result is expected to come on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Five reasons why Best was a genius
headlines.description: George Best would have been 80 on 22 May. Take a look back on an unforgettable career and enjoy five of his most memorable goals.
